key_insight: CoTAR reconstructs molecular topology, formal charges, and unpaired electrons from condensed-phase trajectories by combining a GNN with HMM-based temporal smoothing and chemical priors.
engineering_relevance: It makes universal ML interatomic potentials easier to analyze and reconnect to classical force fields, enabling bond-aware downstream MD workflows from atomistic simulation data.
method: Builds a proximity graph from atomic species and coordinates and applies message passing with a van der Waals prior.
Uses chemical constraints plus a hidden Markov model to smooth topology predictions over time.
Infers bonds, formal charges, and unpaired electrons from total charge and local atomic environments.
evidence_signal: Across 128 topology-preserving condensed-phase systems, CoTAR reached a bond-order-weighted F1 score of 0.906 on classical-MD data, and few-shot fine-tuning raised valid-snapshot rate on uMLIP trajectories from 38.6% to 84.7%.
watch_item: Performance depends on topology-preserving, nonreactive systems, so robustness on strongly reactive or bond-breaking trajectories may be limited.
paper_title: CoTAR: Topology and Atomic State Reconstruction in Condensed Phases
